question:
Solve the following arithmetic problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

Trevor is currently 11 years old and his older brother is 20 years old. How old will Trevor be when his older brother is three times as old as Trevor is now?
response:
Okay, so Trevor is 11 now and his brother is 20. The question asks when the brother will be three times as old as Trevor is NOW, so that means three times 11, which is 33.

So I need to figure out when the brother will be 33 years old. If he's 20 now, then he needs to age 13 more years to get to 33. That means Trevor will also age 13 years, so Trevor will be 11 + 13 = 24 years old.

Wait, let me check that... when Trevor is 24, his brother will be 33. That seems right!
